ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: They went to Bob…î town for the funeral ceremony; and the children also went with their parents to give them advice.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: And they said that Azile and Mawu Elkosakposa came to her and she gave birth to a child named Amrah. Bebava Keena Azile and Kosoti Baye Bede, and Mebaya Nyamenle.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: The wise one's word is a parable they spoke, When he understands the true path is good, He said to my people, "Edenkema comes in the land of the living there." And when he comes, he will not be able to find him, for he has already searched my path there.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na m…î…î nrenya ne nwunle k…õ yeangola yeangomo Gyek…îbo anwo zo la, …îvale …î sa …îhanle Gyek…îbo …õz…îl…õ, na Gyek…îbo adwongu huanenle w…î …õfutul…õ ne anu.
translated: Moo taught the children to learn, and Gyekobo arrived there. He saw that Gyekobo had departed, and Gyekobo came back and returned afterwards.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yem…îti Nyamenle b…île sonla k…õ …îdaye ye …õsaso la; …îdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye …õsaso a …înleanle …îb…île sonla a. ∆Üb…île b…õ nrenya nee raal…õ.
translated: The Word of the Merciful One is true; His works are just. The Merciful One's abundant deeds and His fulfillment of His true Word shall endure forever.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: Yem…îti did evil in …õl…õnemgbole; and the ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli came and caused nuhua with the people of amuala.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: And I said to him, "The priest and the elders have come to us." Then the priest said to them, "Go to the town and prepare, and we will go to Nyamenle."

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: He spoke the word from his mouth, then the heavy rain fell, I worked during the day, Edinkema stayed in the chief's house. And when the person came, he could not see the child because he had taken my belongings elsewhere.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na m…î…î nrenya ne nwunle k…õ yeangola yeangomo Gyek…îbo anwo zo la, …îvale …î sa …îhanle Gyek…îbo …õz…îl…õ, na Gyek…îbo adwongu huanenle w…î …õfutul…õ ne anu.
translated: And he sent his words to go and call Gyek…îbo, they went there; then he spoke the words Gyek…îbo had asked, and Gyek…îbo sent to inquire about the matter with them.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yem…îti Nyamenle b…île sonla k…õ …îdaye ye …õsaso la; …îdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye …õsaso a …înleanle …îb…île sonla a. ∆Üb…île b…õ nrenya nee raal…õ.
translated: They sent Nyamenle's first word so that he would create the earth there; he created another earth and the earth, and he saw the first word. The word will remain in his place.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: Yem…îti performed the role of the people's leader, this great name; and they repeatedly spoke and spoke that they and the knowledge of the truth.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: And he said to the man Az…õl…õ, "Come to Meamaa in his land, for those people." He also said to King Keena, "Carry it out well, and I will give it to Nyamenle."

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: Then after he had done these things, he went to the region of Donoyara with his disciples, and he called his disciples and spoke the word of God to them. And when he had finished, he sent them away to return to their homes.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na m…î…î nrenya ne nwunle k…õ yeangola yeangomo Gyek…îbo anwo zo la, …îvale …î sa …îhanle Gyek…îbo …õz…îl…õ, na Gyek…îbo adwongu huanenle w…î …õfutul…õ ne anu.
translated: Then he went with his people to the place where Gyek…îbo was staying, he went and informed Gyek…îbo, and Gyek…îbo came to meet them at the appointed time.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yem…îti Nyamenle b…île sonla k…õ …îdaye ye …õsaso la; …îdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye …õsaso a …înleanle …îb…île sonla a. ∆Üb…île b…õ nrenya nee raal…õ.
translated: Blessed is Nyamenle whose word is truth; his work is eternal. As he speaks with his mouth, so his work is done with truth. His words extend from heaven to earth.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: Yem…îti teaches the children the value of a good name; and they work diligently to accumulate wealth.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: And he said to them, "This is the word of the Lord which he has spoken to you, saying, I will make you a great nation. He said to them, 'This is the covenant which I have made with you, and I will be your God.'"

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: When the child grew up, he went to live in the wilderness. Later, he came out of the wilderness to take his father's name, and Edenkema was situated on the other side of the river. Then, when he was there, they brought a ram for him to sacrifice for the sins of the people.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na m…î…î nrenya ne nwunle k…õ yeangola yeangomo Gyek…îbo anwo zo la, …îvale …î sa …îhanle Gyek…îbo …õz…îl…õ, na Gyek…îbo adwongu huanenle w…î …õfutul…õ ne anu.
translated: And he saw the child and his mother when they were in the town of Gyek…îbo there, he went there to where Gyek…îbo was staying, and Gyek…îbo returned with the child to the house and his family.
